project: RPS-01: Chain-of-Thought LLM for Spatial Analysis — Kamrup Flood Dataset
data_status: REAL: District shapefile (Assam_Districts_Shapefiles), Roads/Rivers/Villages/LandUse (from Overpass). SYNTHETIC: All raster layers + fault lines + population grid + flood records

kamrup_dem_30m.asc.format: ESRI ASCII Grid (load in QGIS as raster)
kamrup_dem_30m.asc.description: Digital Elevation Model — Brahmaputra floodplain (~20-60m), Meghalaya foothills (~60-200m)
kamrup_dem_30m.asc.model_use: Primary flood risk input — low elevation = high inundation probability

kamrup_slope.asc.description: Slope derived from DEM. Floodplain 0-2°, hillslopes 5-25°
kamrup_slope.asc.model_use: Slope inversely correlated with flood pooling depth

kamrup_elevation_class.asc.model_use: Categorical feature for ML flood zone classification

kamrup_rainfall_annual_mm.asc.description: Mean annual rainfall. Higher in south due to Meghalaya orographic effect (1600-2200mm)
kamrup_rainfall_annual_mm.asc.model_use: Spatial rainfall predictor for flood frequency model

kamrup_temperature_mean_c.asc.description: Mean annual temperature. 22-26°C range. Slight lapse rate northward.
kamrup_temperature_mean_c.asc.model_use: Secondary climate feature for evapotranspiration estimation

kamrup_ndvi_sentinel2.asc.description: NDVI proxy from synthetic Sentinel-2 June 2023. Forest=0.6-0.85, Agri=0.3-0.6, Water/Urban=-0.1-0.2
kamrup_ndvi_sentinel2.asc.model_use: Land cover proxy; low NDVI areas correlate with urban flood risk

kamrup_flood_depth_2022.asc.description: Estimated flood inundation depth during 2022 peak monsoon event. Based on DEM + proximity to Brahmaputra channel.
kamrup_flood_depth_2022.asc.model_use: Ground truth / training label raster for inundation prediction

kamrup_fault_lines.geojson.description: Active and potentially active fault lines — Kopili Fault, Naga Thrust, Brahmaputra Fault Zone etc.
kamrup_fault_lines.geojson.model_use: Seismic hazard co-variate; embankment vulnerability near faults

kamrup_landuse_lulc.geojson.description: 57 LULC polygons — Kharif/Rabi cropland, dense/open forest, waterbody, built-up, fallow, grassland
kamrup_landuse_lulc.geojson.model_use: Land cover intersected with flood depth for damage estimation

kamrup_population_grid.geojson.description: 100 grid cells (~0.1° resolution). Population density peaks near Guwahati fringe.
kamrup_population_grid.geojson.model_use: Exposure assessment — population × flood inundation depth = risk score

Assam_Districts_Shapefiles.shp.description: All 33 Assam districts. Filter by Dist_Name='Kamrup' for project area.

export.json (rivers \u2014 reclip needed.description: Waterway features (river, stream, canal). Bbox is wider than Kamrup — needs clipping.

kamrup_flood_incidents_2010_2023.csv.description: Individual flood events 2010-2023. 29 columns including severity, gauge, blocks affected, damage.
kamrup_flood_incidents_2010_2023.csv.model_use: Primary training data for flood impact prediction

kamrup_annual_flood_summary_2010_2023.cs.description: Yearly aggregated flood statistics 2010-2023 with ENSO phase and rainfall.
kamrup_annual_flood_summary_2010_2023.cs.model_use: Temporal trend analysis; ENSO-flood correlation feature

kamrup_monthly_rainfall_temperature_2010.description: Monthly climate data from Guwahati IMD station 2010-2023. Rainfall, temp, humidity.
kamrup_monthly_rainfall_temperature_2010.model_use: Lag features for flood prediction (e.g. antecedent 3-month rainfall)

kamrup_brahmaputra_gauge_daily_2022.csv.description: Daily Brahmaputra gauge readings May-Oct 2022 from Guwahati station. Danger level = 5.10m.
kamrup_brahmaputra_gauge_daily_2022.csv.model_use: River gauge time series for early warning system training

kamrup_block_vulnerability.csv.description: Block-level vulnerability indicators for 12 Kamrup blocks.
kamrup_block_vulnerability.csv.model_use: Spatial join target — attach vulnerability scores to incident records

kamrup_flood_incidents_spatial.geojson.description: Spatially referenced version of flood_incidents — same data, geocoded by event epicentre.
kamrup_flood_incidents_spatial.geojson.model_use: Spatial clustering, hotspot mapping, QGIS visualisation

rag_ingestion_notes.chunk_strategy: One JSON object per layer (this file) forms one RAG document. Each CSV row = one chunk. Each raster metadata JSON = one chunk.
